narrative.headline: 2022 Toyota Camry: air bags lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 56 complaints with NHTSA. Air bags are the most reported area at 18%, and they also carry the worst outcomes of any group, with 7 crashes and 5 injuries. Service brakes, the electrical system, and forward collision avoidance follow, each at 13%, with vehicle speed control at 11%. No fires or deaths are reported across any area.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: The top complaint: 10 reports (18%), tied to 7 crashes and 5 injuries, the most of any area. Some owners report air bags that did not deploy in a collision.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 7 reports (13%), linked to 2 crashes and 1 injury. Some owners describe pressing the brake pedal and getting little or no response. Test the brakes carefully.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 7 reports (13%), with no crashes or injuries recorded. Owners mention warning lights and sudden loss of power while driving.

issues.sample: Drove a new 2022 Toyota Camry LE with 15 miles. Notified dealer that several safety and infotainment systems were non operational. Vehicle had multiple warning light related to these systems. Notably, the Adaptive Cruise Control, Automatic Emergency Braking, Blind Spot Warning, F
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 7 reports (13%), with 1 crash and 1 injury. Owners report driver-assist features such as automatic emergency braking not working as expected. Confirm these systems work on a test drive.

issues.sample: Drove a new 2022 Toyota Camry LE with 15 miles. Notified dealer that several safety and infotainment systems were non operational. Vehicle had multiple warning light related to these systems. Notably, the Adaptive Cruise Control, Automatic Emergency Braking, Blind Spot Warning, F
issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 6 reports (11%), tied to 2 crashes and 2 injuries. A smaller but recurring area covering how the car manages speed, including adaptive cruise control.
